what_it_does: This file lays out a plan to dogfood the Upward Engine by pointing it at the company's own scattered files, design history, and migration corpus as the first real use case, then using that self-hosted run to validate the UI and sequence further build work.
observations: Proposes a recursive bootstrap where building the engine and using it are the same act
The first corpus is the company's own material, with the hand-made orienteering ledger acting as a check
Recollection should target the design-history corpus that includes the sessions where the engine itself was designed
The UI becomes the look-and-steer surface over the engine's self-understanding
Describes a seed-to-system path: wizard prototype -> composable MCP chains -> UI -> company corpus -> generalised loop
Sequences development as run-monitor/multi-space map first, then embedding distilled units, then chain composer, then marks/ratify, then union with session_recall
Ends with an unresolved open question about whether the engine map replaces the hand ledger or runs beside it
standouts: The use and the build are the same act
The company's own corpus is the safest first ground truth because wrong reconstructions are cheap and feedback is immediate
The hand-made orienteering ledger is framed as both v0 prototype and acceptance test for the engine's map
conventions: Uses frontmatter with type concept, register descriptive, aliases, tags, status draft, and relates-to wikilinks
Follows a heading-and-bullet structure with a blockquoted open question
Names internal components with backtick code formatting and wikilink references
Explicitly marks status as draft and includes an open question
concerns: Draft status leaves many specifics uncommitted
The plan depends on future components (MCP units, marks/ratify tier, VRAM greenlight) that are not defined in this file
The acceptance-test relationship between engine map and hand ledger is stated but has no concrete criteria
notes: A draft concept that argues the Upward Engine should first eat its own company corpus to validate the UI and find the next build walls.
questions: What exactly counts as 'the company's own scattered self' for the first corpus?
How is agreement between the engine map and the hand-made orienteering ledger measured?
What are the concrete acceptance criteria for the hand ledger becoming a check?
What does the marks/ratify tier specifically propose and confirm?
What triggers the shift from 'beside' to 'supersede' for the hand ledger?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is a dogfooding plan and delivers a sequencing strategy, rationale, and open question on exactly that topic.
intention_signals: It is trying to justify and sequence a self-hosting bootstrap where the engine's first real use is understanding the company that is building it, turning that loop into both product validation and a development roadmap.
novelty: It treats the company's hand-made orienteering ledger as both the v0 version of the engine's output and the acceptance test for the engine's automated reconstruction, making the bootstrap recursive.
contribution: A concrete dogfooding strategy that ties the engine's first corpus, UI validation, and build sequencing to a known-ground-truth body of company material.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a draft concept document outlining the Upward Engine's dogfooding plan: applying the engine to the company's own files and design history as the first real corpus and using the resulting map to validate the UI and guide build sequencing.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to define a self-hosting bootstrap path where building the engine and using it on the company corpus are the same act.
